name: Swedish Christmas Sausage (Korv)
recipeIngredient: 3 lbs lean beef, ground together with 3 lbs lean pork
1 tbsp ground pepper (seasoned pepper is good)
a pinch of bay leaves, chopped
2 ½ cups beef or pork stock or scalded milk, cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Get hog casings from your butcher. You may need to order them ahead of time. Pick up the casings the day before you make the sausage and soak them overnight in a mild salt brine in your refrigerator. Rinse with cold water before using.
2. Make mashed potatoes (packaged are fine). Chop onions finely and sauté in a little Crisco. Do not let brown. Cool. Mix meat, mashed potatoes, onions, allspice, salt, pepper, bay leaves, oregano, powdered cloves, and stock very thoroughly.
3. Place a little vegetable oil on the spout of the grinder or sausage machine. Fry a pinch of the mixture in Crisco to test the flavor. Add more seasonings if needed. The flavor of allspice is important but should be subtle. The mixture should be light. Add more stock if needed.
4. Rinse casings in cold water and cut into 16-inch pieces (approximately). Tie one end of each section. Fill the grinder or sausage machine with meat mixture. Ease the end of the casing about 2 inches onto the spout. Turn the handle slowly. Stop turning when you are 1 ½ inches from the end of the casing. Do not pack the sausage casing too tightly. Remove from the spout and tie the second end. Put in Baggies and freeze or refrigerate in a mild salt brine with saltpeter, not more than 4 or 5 days.
5. To cook, defrost and set the oven at 250 degrees. In an open shallow pan, add 2 tablespoons of Crisco and ½ cup water (or just ½ cup water). Place sausages in the pan and cook for 45 minutes.
